response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more than Emily which makes his total 39 blueberries. Additionally David gathered three times as many blueberries as Sophia. This means Sophia gathered 13 blueberries. Together they have 24 plus 39 plus 13 blueberries. Their total collection is 76 blueberries.

***

They used all the blueberries to make muffins. Each batch of muffins requires 9 blueberries. To find out how many batches they made they divided the total blueberries by 9. This calculation gives them 8 full batches with some blueberries left over. They sold each batch for $5. Multiplying the number of batches by the price per batch gives the total money made.

***

The calculation for the total money made is 8 multiplied by 5. This results in 40 dollars. They used all their blueberries to make the muffins and sold them at a price of 5 dollars per batch. The leftover blueberries were not used for making more batches. They were not sold or used in any other way.

***

The total money they made from selling the muffins is 40 dollars. They used all their collected blueberries to make 8 batches. Each batch was sold for 5 dollars. Their effort and collection led to a total of 40 dollars. The youth of the group was evident in their work and the outcome.
